name: Mistlands
description:
Mistlands

Key Demographics: Humans and Dwarves

Capital City: Payden's Port

Entanglements

To the North:
The Violet Steppe (Ykrul) – Winter raids sometimes pierce the fog, but the mist is a better guardian than any wall. The Ykrul do not linger where the bells ring false.
To the South:
Aeler – Mistland caravans pass each other, carrying arms and bells one way, grain and uncertain futures, the other.
To the East: (via the Aberderrin)
The Linns – Longship raids come when the bell-lines are weak; the sea is the second border, and it does not ring. The fog-wardens watch the horizon as much as the bells.
To the West:
The mist rolls in from the heart of the wood, carrying whispers, wraiths, and death.
Theme & Atmosphere

The Mistlands lie north of the Aelerian mountains, where the Aberderrin Sea laps against reed-fens and drowned shores. To the west, the Direwood breathes; to the east, the salt tides of the Aberderrin. It is a Protectorate of the Aeler, but their iron passes only hold the southern roads — the land itself belongs to fog and bells. Every levee hums with ward-notes, every ferry takes salt as tithe, and every shadow is measured against the Direwood's endless breath. The people here live by the weight of salt, the echo of bells, and the hope that their names will not be spoken by the mists.

The Aeler Protectorate maintains order with patrols, garrisons, and breath-bonds, but the real law is older: salt lines drawn at dusk, iron nails hammered into threshold posts, and the unspoken knowledge that the ninth bell must never ring. The Mistlands do not forgive forgetfulness. The mist has a long memory, and it shares its grudges freely.

What you notice first: The constant, distant chime of bells — never quite in sync. The taste of salt on your lips even inland. The way everyone holds their breath when crossing a bridge. The silence when a bell fails to ring.

The one rule that kills newcomers: Never answer a voice that calls your name from the fog. The mist collects names, and once it has yours, the ferryman will always know where to find you.

Regional Mood: Bells and Breath

Here, breath is currency and silence a sin. Every exhalation is counted by the ward-bells, and every stolen inhalation feeds something that should not be. The Aeler Protectorate maintains order with patrols and garrisons, but the real law is older: salt lines drawn at dusk, iron nails hammered into threshold posts, and the unspoken knowledge that the ninth bell must never ring. The Mistlands do not forgive forgetfulness. The mist has a long memory, and it shares its grudges freely.

The tension between the Aeler governor (Legate Halvar) and the local bell-wardens is constant. The Aeler want standardized bell-codes, the salt-tithe weighed true, and villages who keep the old pacts. The wardens want the old ways: counting in eights, never the ninth, and the right to refuse a name to the fog. Payden's Port, the only true city, is a battleground of writs and lanterns. Grain ships leave each autumn, carrying the Protectorate's due east and returning with iron, engineers, and the occasional missionary — who rarely stay.

Prominent NPCs of the Mistlands

|p0.25|p0.3|p0.15|}

Name

Role/TitleMotivationRumoured Quirk
Legate HalvarAeler governor of the ProtectorateKeep the bell-lines intact and the grain flowing eastShe carries a bell that has never been rung. It will ring once, at the end of her reign.
Mother AnjaSalt-Monk of the White PansPreserve the old wards against the DirewoodHer tongue is white with salt; she speaks only in blessings or silence.
Warden LucasBell-Warden of the High LineProtect his village from the mist and the Aeler law-menHe refuses to write down the ninth note; it lives only in his fading memory.
Sister DaryaHealer of the Gray Hospice of the Unnamed (Temple of Light)Tend the fog-sick and the drownedHer gray robes never stain, and she asks no payment — only a name to carry in her keeping.
Notary VossCovenant judge (Mykkiel)Impose Aeler law on local customsHe carries a water-timer to measure the shifting of legal moods. The mist ignores it.
The FerrymanOath-ferryman of the Ghost SlipGather tolls in salt and whispered namesHis left hand is always gloved; underneath, the names are carved into his palm.

Names of the Mistlands

Inspired by the coastal fens and the Aeler protectorate: clipped, breathy, with soft vowels and hard endings. Surnames often denote a marsh, a craft, or a ward.

(Fen/Levee/Bell-line)

Reed-fen causeway with chalked ward-runes on every milestone; Bell-Line levee with iron posts and eight bells per span.
